rules.risk: Copy/format operations without obvious bounds guards can overflow buffers.
rules.action: Use bounded APIs and explicit length validation from trusted sources.

rules.risk: Freed object may still be reachable in later paths, enabling use-after-free.
rules.action: Null out references, enforce ownership, and validate post-free access paths.

rules.risk: Potential unchecked pointer/function-pointer call can crash under null state.
rules.action: Guard pointer and callback fields before dereference/invocation.

rules.risk: Unchecked multiplication can overflow and under-allocate downstream buffers.
rules.action: Use overflow-check helpers before allocation/index arithmetic.

rules.risk: Locking changes may introduce deadlocks/order inversion without paired review.
rules.action: Verify lock ordering and ensure all exits release locks.

rules.risk: Lock acquisition without visible release can deadlock runtime paths.
rules.action: Ensure paired unlock on all return/error paths.

rules.risk: RCU dereference without read-side protection can race with free/update.
rules.action: Wrap RCU dereference iteration in proper read-side lock scope.

rules.risk: User boundary copy without explicit validation can propagate invalid pointers/lengths.
rules.action: Validate pointer and size; handle partial-copy failures explicitly.

rules.risk: Payload parsing without explicit endianness conversion causes cross-platform misbehavior.
rules.action: Decode protocol fields at the boundary using explicit endian helpers.

rules.risk: Parsing buffers without explicit length checks risks out-of-bounds reads/writes.
rules.action: Gate parsing with strict minimum/maximum length checks before dereference.

rules.risk: Default success codes can mask callback/operation failures.
rules.action: Propagate concrete error results and map them to protocol-visible codes.

rules.risk: Unused API parameters imply incomplete validation and weak contracts.
rules.action: Use parameter in validation logic or remove it from interface.

rules.risk: Logging sensitive payload/auth values can expose secrets in logs.
rules.action: Redact sensitive fields and avoid raw payload/credential logging.

rules.risk: Auth/authz bypass toggles can disable security controls in production paths.
rules.action: Enforce deny-by-default authorization and isolate test-only bypass logic.

rules.risk: New API/service blocks without grouping docs reduce discoverability and consistency.
rules.action: Add/update nearby \defgroup docs for the introduced service/API family.

rules.risk: API changes without test updates can ship silent regressions.
rules.action: Add API-level regression tests for changed interface and failure behavior.

rules.risk: Consumers may miss migration or behavioral impact without release notes.
rules.action: Add release-note/changelog entry covering compatibility and migration impact.

rules.risk: Allocation-heavy changes without visible release paths can leak memory over time.
rules.action: Confirm ownership and teardown/free paths for new allocations.

rules.risk: Reference increments without matching put/dec in touched paths can leak objects or mask UAF.
rules.action: Verify refcount symmetry for all success and error paths.

rules.risk: Multiple frees of the same pointer can corrupt allocator metadata and crash runtime.
rules.action: Guard free paths and null/reset ownership after free.

rules.risk: Potential non-terminating loops can cause hang/DoS under error conditions.
rules.action: Add explicit exit conditions, timeout guards, or watchdog-safe breaks.

rules.risk: Shared-structure mutation without visible synchronization can introduce data races.
rules.action: Protect shared updates with appropriate lock/RCU discipline.

rules.risk: Nested lock acquisition can introduce lock-order inversions and deadlocks.
rules.action: Validate lock ordering and document nesting constraints explicitly.

rules.risk: Division without denominator validation can trigger runtime faults.
rules.action: Add explicit non-zero denominator checks before division.

rules.risk: Signedness mismatch checks can be ineffective and hide boundary bugs.
rules.action: Use consistent signed/unsigned types and explicit conversion/validation.

rules.risk: Raw pointer/address logging can leak sensitive kernel layout information.
rules.action: Use hardened pointer formats and avoid exposing raw addresses.

rules.risk: Fallible operations without explicit result checks can propagate silent corruption.
rules.action: Capture and validate return values; handle failures immediately.

rules.risk: Inconsistent callback/function naming can mislead reviewers and maintainers about semantics.
rules.action: Align callback and service-handler naming with existing subsystem conventions.
